content.problemStatement:
In a faraway kingdom there are NNN cities numbered 111 to NNN connected to each other in the form of a tree. The city numbered 111 is the kingdom where the king resides. A city is called a leaf if it is not the kingdom and it has exactly one adjacent city. Each leaf city has an assassin.

The enemies of the king have announced a huge reward for the assassination of the king. Each assassin starts moving from his current city following the shortest path to the kingdom. It takes exactly 111 day for an assassin to move to an adjacent city..

The assassins don’t want to split the reward, so if an assassin AAA encounters any other assassin BBB ahead of him on the same path that AAA is supposed to follow (no matter how far ahead), then AAA uses his magical power to attack BBB.

After all the magical powers were used, every assassin who was attacked dies. Suppose AAA is a living assassin who attacked somebody. Now, AAA will move directly to the position of the assassin closest to the kingdom that AAA attacked, with the cost of 111 day. If there are multiple assassins in the same city simultaneously, they form a pact not to kill each other and start moving together. **Once an assassin reaches the kingdom, he cannot be killed anymore**, since he can kill the king, take the reward, and leave.

Your task is to find which assassin will reach the kingdom first and in how many days. If more than one assassin reaches the kingdom simultaneously, output all of them in sorted order. Each assassin is represented by the number of the city where they started from.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases
The first line of every test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of cities.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two integers uuu and vvv denoting that city uuu is connected to city vvv.
###Output


For every test case, the first line should contain KKK and TTT, the number of assassins who are first to reach and the number of days required.
In the next line print KKK space-separated integers denoting the assassins who are first to reach the kingdom in sorted order.
###Constraints


1≤T≤1051\leq T\leq10^51≤T≤105
3≤N≤2⋅1053\leq N\leq2\cdot 10^53≤N≤2⋅105
1≤u,v≤N1\leq u, v\leq N1≤u,v≤N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
The connections form a tree structure.
###Subtasks
**Subtask #1 (30 points):**


T≤103T \leq 10^3T≤103
N≤2⋅103N \leq 2\cdot 10^3N≤2⋅103
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
Time limit: 111 sec
**Subtask #2 (70 points):**


Original Constraints
Time limit: 222 sec

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 :
The assassins are initially present at nodes 444, 777, 888, 999, and 101010 (consider it day 000). On day 111, their positions become:

Assassin 444 => Node 222
Assassin 777 => Node 333
Assassin 888 => Node 555
Assassin 999 => Node 666
Assassin 101010 => Node 666

Now, since assassin 444 is in the same path as assassin 888, and assassin 777 is in the same path as assassin 999 and assassin 101010, they both will be assassinated and assassin 888 will get to reach node 222 at cost of 111 day (no benefit) and assassin 999 and 101010 will also reach node 333 in one day (which is Day 222). Now assassin 888 is on node 222 and assassin 999 and 101010 are at node 333, all three of them reach node 111 simultaneously so all three of assassin 888, assassin 999 and assassin 101010 win (With a total cost of 333 days).
Test Case 2 :
The assassins are initially present at nodes 333, 666, and 777  on day 000.
On day 111, their positions become:

Assassin 333 => Node 222
Assassin 666 => Node 444
Assassin 777 => Node 555

Now since assassin 333 and assassin 666 are on the path of assassin 777, the assassin 777 will kill both of them and will advance to the position of assassin 333, that is node 222 (nearest position to the kingdom), and this will cost him 111 day. On the next day (day 333), he will reach the kingdom and take the reward.
